title: Bring your own DEM via STAC
text: bundled test DEM 25 m BC TRIM DEM resampled 10 m grid using bilinear interpolation. means original 25 m pixel gets split smaller 10 m pixels, values smoothly blended neighbours. grid genuinely 10 m — cell 10 m × 10 m — terrain detail still 25 m quality. ’s like zooming low-resolution photo: image gets bigger sharper. real topographic detail added. contrast, 1 m lidar tiles STAC catalog natively 1 m. capture real features scale — side channels, terrace edges, drainage ditches — 25 m DEM physically see, matter finely resample .

title: Bring your own DEM via STAC
text: First, load bundled test data run VCA establish baseline.
code: dem_10m <- rast(system.file("testdata/dem.tif", package = "flooded")) slope_10m <- rast(system.file("testdata/slope.tif", package = "flooded")) streams <- st_read(   system.file("testdata/streams.gpkg", package = "flooded"),   quiet = TRUE )  precip_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem_10m, field = "map_upstream")  valleys_10m <- fl_valley_confine(   dem_10m, streams,   field = "upstream_area_ha",   slope = slope_10m,   slope_threshold = 9,   max_width = 2000,   cost_threshold = 2500,   flood_factor = 6,   precip = precip_r )  n_10m <- sum(values(valleys_10m) == 1, na.rm = TRUE) cat("10 m DEM valley cells:", n_10m, "/", ncell(valleys_10m),     "(", round(100 * n_10m / ncell(valleys_10m), 1), "%)\n") #> 10 m DEM valley cells: 54637 / 518400 ( 10.5 %)

what: Fetch 1 m lidar DEM from STAC
title: Bring your own DEM via STAC
text: Query stac-dem-bc collection tiles intersecting test area. filter 2019 tiles (1 m lidar).
code: # Test area bounding box in WGS84 # terra ext() gives xmin,xmax,ymin,ymax — STAC needs xmin,ymin,xmax,ymax dem_wgs <- project(dem_10m, "EPSG:4326") e <- ext(dem_wgs) bbox <- c(e$xmin, e$ymin, e$xmax, e$ymax)  # Query STAC items <- stac("https://images.a11s.one/") |>   stac_search(     collections = "stac-dem-bc",     bbox = bbox,     datetime = "2019-01-01T00:00:00Z/2019-12-31T23:59:59Z"   ) |>   post_request() |>   items_fetch()  cat("STAC items found:", length(items$features), "\n") #> STAC items found: 2 for (f in items$features) cat(" ", f$id, "\n") #>   093-093l-2019-dem-bc_093l059_xli1m_utm09_2019  #>   093-093l-2019-dem-bc_093l049_xli1m_utm09_2019

title: Bring your own DEM via STAC
text: Use gdalcubes mosaic STAC tiles reproject BC Albers (EPSG:3005), matching test area extent. use 5 m resolution practical compromise — finer bundled 10 m, fast enough build vignette. production work, set dx = 1, dy = 1 full 1 m resolution.
code: # Build gdalcubes image collection from STAC items col <- stac_image_collection(items$features, asset_names = "image")  # Define target cube — same extent as bundled DEM, 5 m resolution e <- ext(dem_10m) stac_res <- 5  # use 1 for full resolution (100x more cells, ~25 min fetch) v <- cube_view(   srs = "EPSG:3005",   extent = list(     left = e$xmin, right = e$xmax,     bottom = e$ymin, top = e$ymax,     t0 = "2019-01-01", t1 = "2019-12-31"   ),   dx = stac_res, dy = stac_res, dt = "P1Y",   aggregation = "first",   resampling = "bilinear" )  # write_tif creates a directory with timestamped TIF(s) inside cube <- raster_cube(col, v) out_dir <- tempfile() write_tif(cube, out_dir) dem_stac_path <- list.files(out_dir, pattern = "\\.tif$", full.names = TRUE)[1] dem_stac <- rast(dem_stac_path)  cat(stac_res, "m DEM:", ncol(dem_stac), "x", nrow(dem_stac), "pixels\n") #> 5 m DEM: 1600 x 1296 pixels cat("Resolution:", res(dem_stac), "m\n") #> Resolution: 5 5 m

title: Bring your own DEM via STAC
code: slope_stac <- terra::terrain(dem_stac, v = "slope", unit = "degrees") # Convert to percent slope to match flooded convention slope_stac <- tan(slope_stac * pi / 180) * 100

title: Bring your own DEM via STAC
code: # Rasterize streams onto the STAC DEM grid precip_stac <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem_stac, field = "map_upstream")  valleys_stac <- fl_valley_confine(   dem_stac, streams,   field = "upstream_area_ha",   slope = slope_stac,   slope_threshold = 9,   max_width = 2000,   cost_threshold = 2500,   flood_factor = 6,   precip = precip_stac )  n_stac <- sum(values(valleys_stac) == 1, na.rm = TRUE) cat(stac_res, "m DEM valley cells:", n_stac, "/", ncell(valleys_stac),     "(", round(100 * n_stac / ncell(valleys_stac), 1), "%)\n") #> 5 m DEM valley cells: 250564 / 2073600 ( 12.1 %)

title: Bring your own DEM via STAC
text: Valley delineation bundled 10 m DEM (top) vs STAC lidar DEM (bottom).
code: # Resample STAC result to 10 m grid for visual comparison valleys_stac_10 <- resample(valleys_stac, dem_10m, method = "near")  # Area comparison area_10m <- n_10m * res(dem_10m)[1] * res(dem_10m)[2] / 1e6 area_stac <- sum(values(valleys_stac_10) == 1, na.rm = TRUE) *   res(dem_10m)[1] * res(dem_10m)[2] / 1e6  cat("Valley area (bundled 10 m DEM):", round(area_10m, 2), "km²\n") #> Valley area (bundled 10 m DEM): 5.46 km² cat("Valley area (STAC", stac_res, "m DEM):", round(area_stac, 2), "km²\n") #> Valley area (STAC 5 m DEM): 6.26 km² par(mfrow = c(2, 1), mar = c(2, 4, 2, 1)) plot(valleys_10m, col = c("grey90", "darkgreen"),      main = "Bundled 10 m DEM (25 m TRIM resampled)", legend = FALSE) plot(st_geometry(streams), add = TRUE, col = "blue", lwd = 1)  plot(valleys_stac_10, col = c("grey90", "darkgreen"),      main = paste0("STAC lidar ", stac_res, " m DEM"), legend = FALSE) plot(st_geometry(streams), add = TRUE, col = "blue", lwd = 1)

title: Bring your own DEM via STAC
text: watershed scale, 5–10 m resolution practical. site-level restoration prescriptions — identifying excavate historic fill, reconnect side channels, plug drainage trenches — 1 m lidar essential. features invisible 25 m. crop ~3.5 × 4 km site around Robert Hatch Creek Richfield Creek enter Bulkley River floodplain, run full 1 m resolution. Site-level comparison: resampled 10 m (top) vs native 1 m lidar (bottom). Fine-scale features like side channels terrace edges emerge 1 m.
code:
# Site extent — Robert Hatch / Richfield confluence with Bulkley site_ext <- ext(976560, 980060, 1055808, 1059808) # Fetch 1 m lidar for the site extent site_wgs <- project(rast(site_ext, crs = "EPSG:3005"), "EPSG:4326") se <- ext(site_wgs) site_bbox <- c(se$xmin, se$ymin, se$xmax, se$ymax)  site_items <- stac("https://images.a11s.one/") |>   stac_search(     collections = "stac-dem-bc",     bbox = site_bbox,     datetime = "2019-01-01T00:00:00Z/2019-12-31T23:59:59Z"   ) |>   post_request() |>   items_fetch()  cat("STAC tiles for site:", length(site_items$features), "
") #> STAC tiles for site: 2  # Mosaic at 1 m site_col <- stac_image_collection(site_items$features, asset_names = "image") site_view <- cube_view(   srs = "EPSG:3005",   extent = list(     left = 976560, right = 980060,     bottom = 1055808, top = 1059808,     t0 = "2019-01-01", t1 = "2019-12-31"   ),   dx = 1, dy = 1, dt = "P1Y",   aggregation = "first",   resampling = "bilinear" )  site_cube <- raster_cube(site_col, site_view) site_dir <- tempfile() write_tif(site_cube, site_dir) dem_1m <- rast(list.files(site_dir, "\.tif$", full.names = TRUE)[1])  cat("1 m DEM:", ncol(dem_1m), "x", nrow(dem_1m), "pixels (",     format(ncell(dem_1m), big.mark = ","), "cells)
") #> 1 m DEM: 3500 x 4000 pixels ( 1.4e+07 cells) slope_1m <- terra::terrain(dem_1m, v = "slope", unit = "degrees") slope_1m <- tan(slope_1m * pi / 180) * 100  # Crop streams to site streams_site <- st_crop(streams, st_as_sfc(st_bbox(c(   xmin = 976560, ymin = 1055808, xmax = 980060, ymax = 1059808 ), crs = st_crs(streams)))) #> Warning: attribute variables are assumed to be spatially constant throughout #> all geometries  precip_1m <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams_site, dem_1m, field = "map_upstream")  valleys_1m <- fl_valley_confine(   dem_1m, streams_site,   field = "upstream_area_ha",   slope = slope_1m,   slope_threshold = 9,   max_width = 2000,   cost_threshold = 2500,   flood_factor = 6,   precip = precip_1m ) #> Warning: [costDist] distance algorithm did not converge  n_1m <- sum(values(valleys_1m) == 1, na.rm = TRUE) cat("1 m DEM valley cells:", format(n_1m, big.mark = ","), "/",     format(ncell(valleys_1m), big.mark = ","),     "(", round(100 * n_1m / ncell(valleys_1m), 1), "%)
") #> 1 m DEM valley cells: 3,883,179 / 1.4e+07 ( 27.7 %) # Run 10 m (resampled TRIM) on the same site for comparison dem_site_10m <- terra::crop(dem_10m, site_ext) slope_site_10m <- terra::crop(slope_10m, site_ext) precip_site_10m <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams_site, dem_site_10m, field = "map_upstream")  valleys_site_10m <- fl_valley_confine(   dem_site_10m, streams_site,   field = "upstream_area_ha",   slope = slope_site_10m,   slope_threshold = 9,   max_width = 2000,   cost_threshold = 2500,   flood_factor = 6,   precip = precip_site_10m )  par(mfrow = c(2, 1), mar = c(2, 4, 2, 1)) plot(valleys_site_10m, col = c("grey90", "darkgreen"),      main = "Site: 10 m (25 m TRIM resampled)", legend = FALSE) plot(st_geometry(streams_site), add = TRUE, col = "blue", lwd = 1)  # Resample 1 m result to same grid for visual comparison valleys_1m_plot <- resample(valleys_1m, dem_site_10m, method = "near") plot(valleys_1m_plot, col = c("grey90", "darkgreen"),      main = "Site: 1 m native lidar", legend = FALSE) plot(st_geometry(streams_site), add = TRUE, col = "blue", lwd = 1)

title: Bring your own DEM via STAC
text: elevated features — “pop-ups” — areas 25 m TRIM DEM classifies floodplain 1 m lidar reveals sitting flood surface. barriers: roads, dykes, fill, raised features block lateral connectivity.
code: # Resample 1 m result to the 25 m (resampled to 10 m) site grid valleys_1m_on_10m <- resample(valleys_1m, dem_site_10m, method = "near")  # Site floodplain area from each DEM cell_area_m2 <- res(dem_site_10m)[1] * res(dem_site_10m)[2]  # 100 m² fp_25m <- sum(values(valleys_site_10m) == 1, na.rm = TRUE) fp_1m  <- sum(values(valleys_1m_on_10m) == 1, na.rm = TRUE)  # "Pop-ups": cells that are floodplain at 25 m but NOT at 1 m popups <- sum(values(valleys_site_10m) == 1 & values(valleys_1m_on_10m) != 1,               na.rm = TRUE)  data.frame(   Metric = c(     "Floodplain area (25 m TRIM)",     "Floodplain area (1 m lidar)",     "Elevated features (pop-ups)",     "Pop-ups as % of 25 m floodplain"   ),   Value = c(     paste(round(fp_25m * cell_area_m2 / 1e4, 1), "ha"),     paste(round(fp_1m * cell_area_m2 / 1e4, 1), "ha"),     paste(round(popups * cell_area_m2 / 1e4, 1), "ha"),     paste0(round(100 * popups / fp_25m, 1), "%")   ) ) |> knitr::kable()

title: Valley confinement from DEM and stream network
text: Burn stream network onto DEM grid (Figure @ref(fig:plot-dem)) using upstream contributing area cell value (Figure @ref(fig:plot-streams)). VCA bankfull regression expects — estimates flood depth contributing area hectares. Rasterized streams coloured upstream area (ha).
code: stream_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "upstream_area_ha") plot(stream_r, main = "Upstream area (ha)")

title: Valley confinement from DEM and stream network
text: VCA bankfull regression precipitation term strongly controls flood depth: precip = 1 (default), precipitation term drops flood depths dramatically underestimated. Bulkley mainstem (upstream_area_ha ~ 110,000), difference ~2 m vs ~8 m flood depth. test data includes map_upstream — mean annual precipitation (mm) fwapg/ClimateBC, carried stream attribute. rasterize alongside streams create spatially varying precipitation surface stream cells.
code: bankfull_width = (upstream_area ^ 0.280) * 0.196 * (precip ^ 0.355) bankfull_depth = bankfull_width ^ 0.607 * 0.145 flood_depth    = bankfull_depth * flood_factor precip_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "map_upstream") cat("Precip at stream cells:", range(values(precip_r), na.rm = TRUE), "mm\n") #> Precip at stream cells: 526 587 mm

title: Valley confinement from DEM and stream network
text: VCA combines four spatial criteria. Let’s inspect one.

title: Valley confinement from DEM and stream network
text: Cells slope <= 9% (VCA default) represent potentially flat valley floor (Figure @ref(fig:plot-slope-mask)). Slope mask: green = slope <= 9%.
code: mask_slope <- fl_mask(slope, threshold = 9, operator = "<=") cat("Gentle slope:", sum(values(mask_slope) == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "cells\n") #> Gentle slope: 280449 cells plot(mask_slope, col = c("grey90", "darkgreen"), main = "Slope <= 9%",      legend = FALSE) plot(st_geometry(streams), add = TRUE, col = "blue", lwd = 0.8)

title: Valley confinement from DEM and stream network
text: Cells within 1000 m stream (half default max_width = 2000; Figure @ref(fig:plot-dist-mask)). Distance mask: corridor within 1 km streams.
code: mask_dist <- fl_mask_distance(stream_r, threshold = 1000) cat("Within 1 km:", sum(values(mask_dist) == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "cells\n") #> Within 1 km: 277943 cells plot(mask_dist, col = c("grey90", "steelblue"), main = "Within 1 km of stream",      legend = FALSE) plot(st_geometry(streams), add = TRUE, col = "blue", lwd = 0.8)

title: Valley confinement from DEM and stream network
text: Accumulated cost (slope-weighted) distance streams (Figure @ref(fig:plot-cost)). VCA default threshold 2500. Cost distance streams. Warm colours = high cost.
code: cost <- fl_cost_distance(slope, stream_r) mask_cost <- fl_mask(cost, threshold = 2500, operator = "<") cat("Cost < 2500:", sum(values(mask_cost) == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "cells\n") #> Cost < 2500: 110827 cells plot(cost, main = "Cost distance", range = c(0, 5000)) plot(st_geometry(streams), add = TRUE, col = "blue", lwd = 0.8)

title: Valley confinement from DEM and stream network
text: bankfull regression estimates flood depth upstream contributing area precipitation, interpolates water surface outward streams, identifies cells flood surface (Figure @ref(fig:plot-flood)). Flood model: depth terrain (m).
code: flood <- fl_flood_model(dem, stream_r, flood_factor = 6, precip = precip_r,                         max_width = 2000) cat("Flooded cells:", sum(values(flood[["flooded"]]) == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "\n") #> Flooded cells: 61845 depth <- flood[["flood_depth"]] depth[depth == 0] <- NA plot(depth, main = "Flood depth (m)") plot(st_geometry(streams), add = TRUE, col = "blue", lwd = 0.8)

title: Valley confinement from DEM and stream network
text: fl_valley_confine() chains (slope + distance + cost + flood masks), applies morphological cleanup (closing, hole filling, small patch removal, majority filter), returns binary valley raster (Figure @ref(fig:plot-valleys)). Note precip argument — without , flood depths ~4x shallow resulting valley significantly narrower. Unconfined valleys (green) stream network.
code: valleys <- fl_valley_confine(   dem, streams,   field = "upstream_area_ha",   slope_threshold = 9,   max_width = 2000,   cost_threshold = 2500,   flood_factor = 6,   precip = precip_r )  n_valley <- sum(values(valleys) == 1, na.rm = TRUE) cat("Valley cells:", n_valley, "/", ncell(valleys),     "(", round(100 * n_valley / ncell(valleys), 1), "%)\n") #> Valley cells: 54418 / 518400 ( 10.5 %) plot(valleys, col = c("grey90", "darkgreen"),      main = "Unconfined valleys", legend = FALSE) plot(st_geometry(streams), add = TRUE, col = "blue", lwd = 1.2)

title: Valley confinement from DEM and stream network
text: Keep valley patches touch stream cell — removes isolated flat areas disconnected network (Figure @ref(fig:plot-connected)). Valley patches connected streams.
code: connected <- fl_patch_conn(valleys, stream_r) cat("Connected valley cells:",     sum(values(connected) == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "\n") #> Connected valley cells: 53585 plot(connected, col = c("grey90", "darkgreen"),      main = "Connected valleys", legend = FALSE) plot(st_geometry(streams), add = TRUE, col = "blue", lwd = 1.2)

title: Valley confinement from DEM and stream network
text: fl_flood_trim() subtracts user-supplied masks. example, trim urban areas, steep terrain, railways, waterbodies. demonstrate removing cells slopes > 5% (stricter threshold).
code: steep_mask <- fl_mask(slope, threshold = 5, operator = ">") trimmed <- fl_flood_trim(connected, steep_mask) cat("After trimming steep cells:",     sum(values(trimmed) == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "\n") #> After trimming steep cells: 36601

title: Valley confinement from DEM and stream network
text: fl_flood_assemble() unions multiple binary rasters. useful combining outputs different flood models data sources.
code: # Example: union the connected valleys with a wider flood-only mask flooded_mask <- flood[["flooded"]] flooded_mask <- ifel(is.na(flooded_mask), 0L, flooded_mask) assembled <- fl_flood_assemble(connected, flooded_mask) cat("Assembled cells:", sum(values(assembled) == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "\n") #> Assembled cells: 63285

title: Valley confinement from DEM and stream network
text: VCA operates terrain alone — lakes wetlands appear donut holes valley raster water surface reads differently surrounding terrain. Similarly, coarse DEM resolution stream channel can sub-pixel excluded output. fl_valley_confine() addresses : channel_buffer — auto-detected streams channel_width column. Buffers stream segment half channel width adds output. DEM correction, habitat buffer. Streams NA channel_width skipped buffer still included VCA flood model via bankfull regression. Users pulling lower-order streams may encounter NA widths — channel width model (Thorley et al., 2021) yet cover first-order streams. waterbodies — optional sf polygon layer lakes wetlands. Rasterized -(buffer) added output via logical . spatial filtering applied — pre-filter valley-bottom features calling headwater waterbodies wanted. Valley delineation without (top) (bottom) waterbodies channel buffer. Dark green = VCA valley floor, blue lines = streams, orange outlines = waterbody polygons (lakes wetlands) added via logical . Waterbodies fill donut holes left terrain-based VCA flat water surfaces read differently surrounding floodplain.
code:
waterbodies <- st_read(   system.file("testdata/waterbodies.gpkg", package = "flooded"),   quiet = TRUE ) cat("Waterbodies:", nrow(waterbodies), "features
") #> Waterbodies: 16 features cat("Types:", paste(names(table(waterbodies$waterbody_type)),                     table(waterbodies$waterbody_type), sep = "=", collapse = ", "), "
") #> Types: L=10, W=6 valleys_wb <- fl_valley_confine(   dem, streams,   field = "upstream_area_ha",   slope = slope,   precip = precip_r,   waterbodies = waterbodies )  n_wb <- sum(values(valleys_wb) == 1, na.rm = TRUE) cell_area <- prod(res(dem)) cat("VCA only:          ", round(n_valley * cell_area / 1e4, 1), "ha
") #> VCA only:           544.2 ha cat("VCA + features:    ", round(n_wb * cell_area / 1e4, 1), "ha
") #> VCA + features:     553.5 ha cat("Features added:    ", round((n_wb - n_valley) * cell_area / 1e4, 1), "ha
") #> Features added:     9.3 ha par(mfrow = c(2, 1), mar = c(2, 4, 2, 1)) plot(valleys, col = c("grey90", "darkgreen"),      main = "VCA only", legend = FALSE) plot(st_geometry(streams), add = TRUE, col = "blue", lwd = 1)  plot(valleys_wb, col = c("grey90", "darkgreen"),      main = "VCA + waterbodies + channel buffer", legend = FALSE) plot(st_geometry(streams), add = TRUE, col = "blue", lwd = 1) if (nrow(waterbodies) > 0) {   plot(st_geometry(waterbodies), add = TRUE, border = "darkorange",        lwd = 1.2) }

title: Valley confinement from DEM and stream network
text: VCA bankfull regression includes precipitation term (precip ^ 0.355) scales flood depth local climate. BC, map_upstream (mean annual precipitation mm) available stream attribute fwapg (Freshwater Atlas Postgres layer). jurisdictions, gridded precipitation product work — rasterize DEM grid pass precip argument. Omitting precipitation (precip = 1, default) underestimates flood depth roughly 4x wet climates (~500 mm MAP). produces valley half width correct result:
code: valleys_no_precip <- fl_valley_confine(   dem, streams, field = "upstream_area_ha",   precip = 1 )  n_no <- sum(values(valleys_no_precip) == 1, na.rm = TRUE) cat("Without precip:", n_no, "cells (",     round(100 * n_no / ncell(dem), 1), "%)\n") #> Without precip: 27522 cells ( 5.3 %) cat("With precip:   ", n_valley, "cells (",     round(100 * n_valley / ncell(dem), 1), "%)\n") #> With precip:    54418 cells ( 10.5 %)

what: Portable Floodplain Delineation from DEM and Stream Network
title: Portable Floodplain Delineation from DEM and Stream Network
text: Portable floodplain delineation DEM stream network. Delineate unconfined valleys floodplain extent using Valley Confinement Algorithm (VCA) — combining slope thresholding, cost-distance analysis, flood surface modelling. Built terra R-native raster processing. Bring DEM streams; works anywhere.

title: Portable Floodplain Delineation from DEM and Stream Network
code: pak::pak("NewGraphEnvironment/flooded")

title: Portable Floodplain Delineation from DEM and Stream Network
text: See Valley confinement vignette full walkthrough bundled test data, function reference individual components.
code: library(flooded)  dem <- terra::rast("dem.tif") streams <- sf::st_read("streams.gpkg")  # delineate valleys — one call valleys <- fl_valley_confine(   dem, streams,   field = "upstream_area_ha",   precip = precip_r  # mean annual precipitation (mm) — critical for depth )  # convert to polygons for GIS valleys_poly <- fl_valley_poly(valleys) sf::st_write(valleys_poly, "valleys.gpkg")

title: Portable Floodplain Delineation from DEM and Stream Network
text: flood model uses VCA bankfull regression estimate flood depth stream cells: upstream_area (hectares) precip (mean annual precipitation, mm) important — omitting precipitation underestimates flood depth ~4x.
code: bankfull_width = (upstream_area ^ 0.280) * 0.196 * (precip ^ 0.355) bankfull_depth = bankfull_width ^ 0.607 * 0.145 flood_depth    = bankfull_depth * flood_factor

what: Accumulated cost distance from stream cells — fl_cost_distance
title: Accumulated cost distance from stream cells — fl_cost_distance
text: Computes least-cost distance every cell nearest stream cell, accumulating friction (typically slope) along path. Stream cells seed points cost zero.

title: Accumulated cost distance from stream cells — fl_cost_distance
code: fl_cost_distance(friction, streams)

title: Accumulated cost distance from stream cells — fl_cost_distance
text: friction SpatRaster movement cost per cell (e.g., percent slope). Higher values = harder traverse. streams SpatRaster rasterized streams (output fl_stream_rasterize()). non-NA cell treated seed point.

title: Accumulated cost distance from stream cells — fl_cost_distance
text: SpatRaster accumulated cost distance. Stream cells value 0; cells increase cost-weighted distance nearest stream.

title: Accumulated cost distance from stream cells — fl_cost_distance
text: Uses terra::costDist() implements push-broom algorithm weighted distance. friction raster defines per-cell traversal cost streams identifies seed cells (cost = 0). Cells NA friction impassable barriers.

title: Accumulated cost distance from stream cells — fl_cost_distance
code: dem <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/dem.tif", package = "flooded")) slope <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/slope.tif", package = "flooded")) streams <- sf::st_read(   system.file("testdata/streams.gpkg", package = "flooded"),   quiet = TRUE ) stream_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "upstream_area_ha") cost <- fl_cost_distance(slope, stream_r) terra::plot(cost, main = "Cost distance from streams", range = c(0, 5000))

what: Interpolate flood surface and compute depth above terrain — fl_flood_depth
title: Interpolate flood surface and compute depth above terrain — fl_flood_depth
text: Takes flood surface elevation stream cells (fl_flood_surface()) interpolates outward produce continuous water surface, subtracts DEM get flood depth. Positive values indicate flooding.

title: Interpolate flood surface and compute depth above terrain — fl_flood_depth
code: fl_flood_depth(dem, flood_surface, max_width = 2000, streams = NULL)

title: Interpolate flood surface and compute depth above terrain — fl_flood_depth
text: dem SpatRaster elevation. flood_surface SpatRaster flood surface elevation stream cells (output fl_flood_surface()). NA non-stream cells. max_width Numeric. Maximum corridor width map units (metres) within interpolate. Default 2000 (1000m side). streams SpatRaster rasterized streams used define interpolation corridor. NULL, derived non-NA cells flood_surface.

title: Interpolate flood surface and compute depth above terrain — fl_flood_depth
text: SpatRaster flood depth (metres terrain). Positive values flooded; 0 stream cells; NA outside corridor depth negative (terrain flood surface).

title: Interpolate flood surface and compute depth above terrain — fl_flood_depth
text: Interpolation uses terra::interpIDW() (inverse distance weighting) propagate flood surface stream cells outward. differs Python VCA uses scipy.interpolate.griddata linear interpolation — IDW available natively terra produces similar results application. interpolation domain limited cells within max_width / 2 nearest stream cell avoid extrapolating distant terrain.

title: Interpolate flood surface and compute depth above terrain — fl_flood_depth
code: dem <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/dem.tif", package = "flooded")) streams <- sf::st_read(   system.file("testdata/streams.gpkg", package = "flooded"),   quiet = TRUE ) stream_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "upstream_area_ha") precip_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "map_upstream") surface <- fl_flood_surface(dem, stream_r, precip = precip_r) depth <- fl_flood_depth(dem, surface, max_width = 2000, streams = stream_r) terra::plot(depth, main = "Flood depth (m)")

what: Run the full flood model — fl_flood_model
title: Run the full flood model — fl_flood_model
text: Convenience wrapper calls fl_flood_surface() fl_flood_depth() sequence, returning multi-layer SpatRaster flood surface elevation, flood depth, binary flooded mask.

title: Run the full flood model — fl_flood_model
code: fl_flood_model(dem, streams, flood_factor = 6, precip = 1, max_width = 2000)

title: Run the full flood model — fl_flood_model
text: dem SpatRaster elevation. streams SpatRaster rasterized streams (output fl_stream_rasterize()). Cell values upstream contributing area hectares (another proxy channel size). flood_factor Numeric. Multiplier bankfull depth estimate flood depth. Default 6 (VCA convention). precip SpatRaster mean annual precipitation (mm), single numeric value applied uniformly. Default 1 (omits precipitation term). max_width Numeric. Maximum corridor width map units (metres) within interpolate. Default 2000 (1000m side).

title: Run the full flood model — fl_flood_model
text: SpatRaster three layers: flood_surface Water surface elevation stream cells (NA elsewhere). flood_depth Depth terrain (metres). 0 streams, NA flooded. flooded Binary mask: 1 flood_depth > 0, 0 streams, NA elsewhere.

title: Run the full flood model — fl_flood_model
code: dem <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/dem.tif", package = "flooded")) streams <- sf::st_read(   system.file("testdata/streams.gpkg", package = "flooded"),   quiet = TRUE ) stream_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "upstream_area_ha") precip_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "map_upstream") flood <- fl_flood_model(dem, stream_r, flood_factor = 6, precip = precip_r)  # Three layers: flood_surface, flood_depth, flooded names(flood) #> [1] "flood_surface" "flood_depth"   "flooded"       terra::plot(flood[["flood_depth"]], main = "Flood depth (m)")

what: Compute flood surface elevation at stream cells — fl_flood_surface
title: Compute flood surface elevation at stream cells — fl_flood_surface
text: Estimates bankfull flood surface elevation stream cell using VCA bankfull regression, adds DEM elevation. result water surface elevation interpolated outward fl_flood_depth().

title: Compute flood surface elevation at stream cells — fl_flood_surface
code: fl_flood_surface(dem, streams, flood_factor = 6, precip = 1)

title: Compute flood surface elevation at stream cells — fl_flood_surface
text: dem SpatRaster elevation. streams SpatRaster rasterized streams (output fl_stream_rasterize()). Cell values upstream contributing area hectares (another proxy channel size). flood_factor Numeric. Multiplier bankfull depth estimate flood depth. Default 6 (VCA convention). precip SpatRaster mean annual precipitation (mm), single numeric value applied uniformly. Default 1 (omits precipitation term).

title: Compute flood surface elevation at stream cells — fl_flood_surface
text: SpatRaster flood surface elevation stream cells NA elsewhere. grid dem.

title: Compute flood surface elevation at stream cells — fl_flood_surface
text: Bankfull regressions follow Valley Confinement Algorithm:   precip = 1 (default), precipitation term drops flood depth depends contributing area. stream raster contains channel width instead contributing area, regression still produces relative flood surface — absolute depth differ spatial pattern preserved.
code: bankfull_width = (upstream_area ^ 0.280) * 0.196 * (precip ^ 0.355) bankfull_depth = bankfull_width ^ 0.607 * 0.145 flood_depth    = bankfull_depth * flood_factor flood_surface  = DEM + flood_depth

title: Compute flood surface elevation at stream cells — fl_flood_surface
code: dem <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/dem.tif", package = "flooded")) streams <- sf::st_read(   system.file("testdata/streams.gpkg", package = "flooded"),   quiet = TRUE ) stream_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "upstream_area_ha") precip_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "map_upstream")  # With precipitation — realistic flood surface surface <- fl_flood_surface(dem, stream_r, flood_factor = 6, precip = precip_r) terra::plot(surface, main = "Flood surface elevation (m)")

title: Rasterize a stream network onto a DEM grid — fl_stream_rasterize
text: streams sf linestring object stream network. template SpatRaster defines output grid (extent, resolution, CRS). Typically DEM. field Character. Column name streams use cell value. Must numeric. Default "channel_width".

title: Rasterize a stream network onto a DEM grid — fl_stream_rasterize
text: SpatRaster grid template. Stream cells carry value field; cells NA.

title: Rasterize a stream network onto a DEM grid — fl_stream_rasterize
text: Rasterization uses terra::rasterize() touches = FALSE (cells whose centre falls stream line burned). multiple features overlap cell, maximum value kept. output CRS matches template. streams template different CRS, streams reprojected match.

title: Rasterize a stream network onto a DEM grid — fl_stream_rasterize
code: dem <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/dem.tif", package = "flooded")) streams <- sf::st_read(   system.file("testdata/streams.gpkg", package = "flooded"),   quiet = TRUE ) stream_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "upstream_area_ha") terra::plot(stream_r, main = "Upstream area (ha)")

what: Delineate unconfined valleys using the Valley Confinement Algorithm — fl_valley_confine
title: Delineate unconfined valleys using the Valley Confinement Algorithm — fl_valley_confine
text: Orchestrates full VCA pipeline: slope thresholding, cost-distance analysis, flood surface modelling, morphological cleanup identify unconfined valley bottoms.

title: Delineate unconfined valleys using the Valley Confinement Algorithm — fl_valley_confine
code: fl_valley_confine(   dem,   streams,   field = "channel_width",   slope = NULL,   slope_threshold = 9,   max_width = 2000,   cost_threshold = 2500,   flood_factor = 6,   precip = 1,   waterbodies = NULL,   channel_buffer = NULL,   size_threshold = 5000,   hole_threshold = 2500 )

title: Delineate unconfined valleys using the Valley Confinement Algorithm — fl_valley_confine
text: dem SpatRaster elevation. streams sf linestring object SpatRaster rasterized streams. sf, rasterized using field. field Character. Column name fl_stream_rasterize() streams sf. Default "channel_width". slope SpatRaster percent slope. NULL, derived dem. slope_threshold Numeric. Maximum percent slope valley floor. Default 9. max_width Numeric. Maximum valley width map units (metres). Default 2000. cost_threshold Numeric. Maximum accumulated cost distance. Default 2500. flood_factor Numeric. Multiplier bankfull depth. Default 6. precip SpatRaster numeric scalar precipitation. Default 1. waterbodies sf polygon object lakes /wetlands, NULL (default). Waterbody polygons rasterized onto valley grid added output morphological cleanup. buffer applied — lake wetland valley part flood system -. spatial filtering applied — polygons rasterized. Pre-filter valley-bottom features calling headwater waterbodies wanted. channel_buffer Logical. Buffer streams channel_width attribute add valley output. Default TRUE streams sf object channel_width column, FALSE otherwise. stream channel floodplain can sub-pixel coarse DEM resolution. size_threshold Numeric. Minimum valley patch area (m²). Default 5000. hole_threshold Numeric. Maximum hole area fill (m²). Default 2500.

title: Delineate unconfined valleys using the Valley Confinement Algorithm — fl_valley_confine
text: SpatRaster binary values: 1 = unconfined valley, 0 = confined / hillslope, NA = outside analysis extent.

title: Delineate unconfined valleys using the Valley Confinement Algorithm — fl_valley_confine
text: algorithm combines four criteria via intersection (): Slope mask — cells slope <= slope_threshold Distance mask — cells within max_width / 2 stream Cost distance mask — cells accumulated cost < cost_threshold Flood mask — cells identified flooded bankfull regression combined mask undergoes morphological cleanup: Closing filter (3x3) bridge small gaps Fill small holes (< hole_threshold) Remove small patches (< size_threshold) Majority filter (3x3) smooth edges cleanup, optional features added via logical : Channel buffer — streams buffered channel_width (DEM correction) Waterbodies — user-supplied lake/wetland polygons rasterized -Adapted USDA Valley Confinement Algorithm Toolbox (BlueGeo implementation Devin Cairns, MIT license) bcfishpass lateral habitat assembly (Simon Norris, Apache 2.0).

title: Delineate unconfined valleys using the Valley Confinement Algorithm — fl_valley_confine
text: Several internal operations (focal filters, distance calculations, raster math) support multi-threading via terra::terraOptions(). Set threads calling function speed processing large rasters:   Apple M4 Max (16 cores), 12 threads reduced runtime ~3.5 minutes ~1 minute 27M-cell raster (~2,700 km² 10 m).
code: terra::terraOptions(threads = 12)

title: Delineate unconfined valleys using the Valley Confinement Algorithm — fl_valley_confine
code: dem <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/dem.tif", package = "flooded")) streams <- sf::st_read(   system.file("testdata/streams.gpkg", package = "flooded"),   quiet = TRUE ) precip_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "map_upstream")  # Basic VCA (channel buffer auto-detected from streams$channel_width) valleys <- fl_valley_confine(   dem, streams,   field = "upstream_area_ha",   precip = precip_r ) terra::plot(valleys, col = c("grey90", "darkgreen"), main = "Unconfined valleys")   # With waterbodies — fills lake/wetland donut holes waterbodies <- sf::st_read(   system.file("testdata/waterbodies.gpkg", package = "flooded"),   quiet = TRUE ) valleys_wb <- fl_valley_confine(   dem, streams,   field = "upstream_area_ha",   precip = precip_r,   waterbodies = waterbodies ) terra::plot(valleys_wb, col = c("grey90", "darkgreen"), main = "With waterbodies")

what: Convert a binary valley raster to polygons — fl_valley_poly
title: Convert a binary valley raster to polygons — fl_valley_poly
text: Polygonizes binary (0/1) valley raster, returning sf polygon layer. Valley cells (1) dissolved contiguous polygons; non-valley cells dropped. natural final step fl_valley_confine().

title: Convert a binary valley raster to polygons — fl_valley_poly
code: fl_valley_poly(x, dissolve = TRUE)

title: Convert a binary valley raster to polygons — fl_valley_poly
text: x SpatRaster binary values (0/1), typically output fl_valley_confine(). dissolve Logical. TRUE (default), adjacent valley cells dissolved multipolygon features. FALSE, contiguous patch separate polygon.

title: Convert a binary valley raster to polygons — fl_valley_poly
text: sf polygon object CRS x. Contains valley column value 1.

title: Convert a binary valley raster to polygons — fl_valley_poly
code: dem <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/dem.tif", package = "flooded")) slope <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/slope.tif", package = "flooded")) gentle <- fl_mask(slope, threshold = 9, operator = "<=") poly <- fl_valley_poly(gentle) terra::plot(dem, main = "Gentle slopes as polygons") plot(sf::st_geometry(poly), add = TRUE, col = "#00800040", border = "darkgreen")

what: flooded: Portable Floodplain Delineation from DEM and Stream Network — flooded-package
title: flooded: Portable Floodplain Delineation from DEM and Stream Network — flooded-package
text: Delineate floodplains digital elevation models stream networks using Valley Confinement Algorithm (VCA). Combines slope thresholding, cost-distance analysis, flood surface modelling identify unconfined valleys. Ported bcfishpass lateral habitat pipeline (Python) origins USDA VCA Toolbox BlueGeo.
